content: Autonomous AI agents can adopt the no-training, no-supervision iterative self-feedback mechanism of Self-Refine to improve their long-horizon task planning capabilities, evolving a "self-correcting agent" paradigm that does not rely on pre-written environment feedback rules or human-labeled interaction data. This paradigm allows agents to adjust their action strategies dynamically by criticizing their own previous planning outputs at each step, solving the common problem of agents deviating from task goals due to rigid pre-set feedback logic.
